title: Microcoelia koehleri
taxonomy: Microcoelia koehleri belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification is due to the plant's orchid-like characteristics and growth patterns. More specifically, Microcoelia koehleri is classified as a genus within the subfamily Vanilleae, which is a group of epiphytic orchids. The subfamily Vanilleae is further divided into several genera, with Microcoelia being one of them. Within the genus Microcoelia, koehleri is a species that is native to Madagascar.
uses: Microcoelia koehleri has been found to have various medicinal properties, although its uses are not well-documented. parts: Microcoelia koehleri is a species of parasitic orchid, and its medicinal parts have been found to have potential uses in traditional medicine. preparations: Microcoelia koehleri. There is limited information available on the herbal preparations of Microcoelia koehleri. intro: Microcoelia koehleri, also known as the "orchid mushroom," is a type of parasitic fungus that belongs to the genus Microcoelia. It is prized for its potential health benefits, including its ability to stimulate the immune system, enhance the body's natural defense mechanisms, and exhibit antiviral and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Microcoelia koehleri include polysaccharides, particularly beta-glucans, which are responsible for its immunomodulatory effects. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations, including capsules, tablets, and tinctures, which are marketed to support immune function and overall well-being.
